scope.target: Draft a first-pass place-order problem frame from existing requirement and spec documents
scope.dependency_radius: Requirement/spec/test-spec files plus new problem-frame artifacts
constraints: Use only repository-local requirement/spec truth as the primary source
Mark deferred runtime concerns instead of inventing retry semantics
Keep the first-pass frame validator-ready and MQ-first aware
non_goals: No claim of full runtime retry or duplicate-handling specification

inputs.architecture_target: Demonstrate that existing repository requirement/spec files can seed first-pass problem frames
review_findings: PlaceOrder already has both use-case spec and integration-aware test specs
Orders and Inventory collaboration is MQ-first and suitable for a CBF example
user_constraints: Continue work from the current git state
Use existing requirement/spec files to produce a real problem-frame draft

steps: Extract the use case contract and collaboration rules from requirement/spec/test-spec files
Map those findings into frame.yaml, machine.yaml, use-case.yaml, aggregate.yaml, and acceptance.yaml
Preserve deferred runtime concerns as explicit gaps rather than invented behavior
validation: The draft references existing requirement/spec truth
The draft captures inventory reservation as a prerequisite for order placement
The draft keeps MQ-only collaboration visible
deferred_items: Retry, duplicate publication, and eventual-consistency timing details
Future contract-level tests for collaboration failure variants

results.summary: Added a first-pass CBF problem frame for Orders.PlaceOrder based on existing requirement, use-case spec, aggregate spec, and test spec files.
